evidence: 129 test files with 1930 tests across 128 suites — substantial coverage volume.
Toolkit layer has strong test coverage: 17 test files in tests/toolkit/tools/ covering all 10 tools plus guards, session, schemas, and state store.
publish-pipeline.ts (actions/) has no dedicated test file — this is the primary publish path for session-runner with DAHR attestation, error guardrails, and indexer health checks.
tips.test.ts, predictions.test.ts, mentions.test.ts test orphaned production code (0 importers) — maintenance effort with no production value.
Test quality enforcement via globalSetup hook rejects assertion-free tests — strong anti-fragility measure.
No integration tests that exercise the session-runner phase sequence end-to-end (even with mocked SDK).

test_strategy.dimension_character: The test portfolio is large and strategically covers the toolkit layer thoroughly — every tool, guard, session, and schema has dedicated tests. The TDD enforcement and assertion-quality hooks are genuinely strong practices. However, there are two strategic gaps: the primary publish pipeline (actions/publish-pipeline.ts) used by session-runner lacks any dedicated tests, and several test files maintain coverage for orphaned production code that has zero importers. The absence of integration-level tests for the session-runner phase orchestration is a notable blind spot.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The toolkit test coverage and quality enforcement push toward 85+. However, the untested publish pipeline is a critical path handling real money operations (DAHR attestation, chain publishing) — a developer modifying that code has no test safety net. The orphaned test files (tips, predictions, mentions) are active maintenance drag. These gaps pull the score into the mid-70s, where a developer would worry about changes in certain areas. Score of 76.2 reflects strong foundations with meaningful strategic holes.

issues.summary: publish-pipeline.ts has no dedicated test file — normalization, error guardrails, and attestation orchestration are untested
evidence: publish-pipeline.ts (200+ LOC) contains attestDahr, attestTlsn, publishPost, normalizeFeedPosts, checkIndexerHealth, encodeHivePost — none have direct test coverage
attestDahr includes DAHR error keyword detection, HTTP status guardrails, XML/HTML rejection, JSON body error scanning — all untested guardrails
This is the primary publish path for session-runner.ts and handles real money operations (chain publishing, attestation)
issues.suggestion: Create tests/actions/publish-pipeline.test.ts with mock SDK. Cover: encodeHivePost encoding, normalizeFeedPosts with various shapes (posts, results, items, data.posts), attestDahr error paths (non-2xx, XML response, JSON error payloads), and publishPost transaction flow.

issues.summary: Test files for orphaned production code — tips.test.ts, predictions.test.ts, mentions.test.ts — waste maintenance effort
evidence: Their test files still run in the test suite consuming CI time and requiring maintenance on vitest upgrades
issues.suggestion: Delete the orphaned production files and their test files together. If the code is needed later, recover from git history.

root_causes: The publish pipeline in actions/ predates the toolkit layer and was never backfilled with tests when TDD was adopted.
Orphaned test files persist because dead-code detection is not integrated into the test suite or CI pipeline.

add.header: Toolkit layer has comprehensive test coverage
add.description: All 10 toolkit tools have dedicated test files, plus guards, session, schemas, state store, URL validator, and security hardening. The toolkit is the best-tested subsystem.

add.header: Test quality enforcement prevents assertion-free tests
add.description: A vitest globalSetup hook and PostToolUse hook reject tests without assertions. This prevents the common pattern of tests that run code but verify nothing.

add.header: No integration tests for session-runner phases
add.description: The 8-phase session-runner orchestration (audit-scan-engage-gate-publish-verify-review-harden) has no end-to-end test, even with mocked dependencies. Phase interactions are only validated in production.
